# Page 1^page=1
COPD Care Loop : Minimal-Friction Scheduling Protocol This protocol is designed to minimize cognitive load and physical exertion for both the patient and caregivers. By standardizing how appointments and medication tasks are entered into the central system, we eliminate the "rushing" triggers that can lead to respiratory spirals. 1. Ap p ointment Entry Standards Every medical appointment must be entered into the Schedules and Tasks system immediately upon scheduling. To ensure a "no-rush" environment, use the following mandatory data ﬁelds: Field Req uirement Detail/Placeholder Primary Driver Who is attending/driving Person Pickup Time Appointment time MINUS 60 mins Date Location Full facility name & address Place Buffer Period 30–60 min pre/post block Reserved for "Breathing Prep" 2. Medication & Pharmacy Integration Reﬁlls and pharmacy tasks should be linked directly to relevant appointments whenever possible to reduce the number of trips. When adding a task related to medications (Maintenance Inhalers, Rescue Inhalers, or Nebulizer supplies), include these details:

# Page 2^page=2
● Pharmacy Name: Place ● Reﬁll Status: Note if the reﬁll is ready for pickup or needs a doctor's authorization. ● Oxygen Supply Check: For every outing, verify the portable oxygen tank levels and LPM requirements. 3. The "No-Sp iral" Buffer Rule The 30–60 minute buffer is a non-negotiable medical necessity. This time is used to: ● Pre-Trip Stabilization: Allow 30 minutes for a "calm start" to prevent shortness of breath (SOB) before leaving the house. ● Post-Trip Recovery: 30–60 minutes of scheduled rest upon returning to monitor O2 saturation and heart rate. ● Trafﬁc Contingency: Ensures that unexpected delays do not cause the anxiety that triggers COPD exacerbations. 4. Documentation & References Use the following links to maintain consistency across the care plan: ● Full Care Plan: TBD ● Vitality Tracker: (Refer to this for daily O2 and BP trends) File ● Emergency Contact: Cody Rice-Velasquez 812.266.1369 This framework was developed to address the web of compounding challenges identiﬁed in June 2025 care logs.
